vocab.example: I've submitted a purchase request for two new laptops.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi đã gửi đề nghị mua sắm cho hai chiếc laptop mới.

vocab.example: Can you get a quotation from at least two vendors?
vocab.example_vi: Bạn có thể xin báo giá từ ít nhất hai nhà cung cấp không?

vocab.example: We calculate depreciation over three years for laptops.
vocab.example_vi: Chúng tôi tính khấu hao trong ba năm đối với laptop.

vocab.example: What's the expected return on investment for this equipment?
vocab.example_vi: Lợi tức đầu tư dự kiến của thiết bị này là bao nhiêu?

vocab.example: We usually compare prices from three different vendors.
vocab.example_vi: Chúng tôi thường so sánh giá từ ba nhà cung cấp khác nhau.

vocab.meaning_vi: chi phí đầu tư tài sản cố định
vocab.example: This purchase counts as capital expenditure, not an expense.
vocab.example_vi: Khoản mua này được tính là chi phí đầu tư tài sản cố định, không phải chi phí thường xuyên.

vocab.meaning_vi: phân tích chi phí - lợi ích
vocab.example: Please attach a cost-benefit analysis to the proposal.
vocab.example_vi: Vui lòng đính kèm bản phân tích chi phí - lợi ích vào đề xuất.

vocab.example: The approval process usually takes about a week.
vocab.example_vi: Quy trình phê duyệt thường mất khoảng một tuần.

listening.title: Proposing new laptops for the design team

lines.speaker: Duc (team lead)
lines.text: Hi Mai, I'd like to submit a purchase request for new laptops.

lines.speaker: Mai (finance manager)
lines.text: Sure, how many laptops does your team need?

lines.speaker: Duc (team lead)
lines.text: Five laptops for the design team. I already have a quotation.

lines.speaker: Mai (finance manager)
lines.text: Great. Which vendor did you get the quotation from?

lines.speaker: Duc (team lead)
lines.text: TechViet Supplies. Their price is lower than the other vendors.

lines.speaker: Mai (finance manager)
lines.text: Good. Is this counted as capital expenditure or a regular expense?

lines.speaker: Duc (team lead)
lines.text: It's capital expenditure, since we'll use them for years.

lines.speaker: Mai (finance manager)
lines.text: Understood. Can you include the depreciation schedule as well?

lines.speaker: Duc (team lead)
lines.text: Yes, and I've also added a cost-benefit analysis to the proposal.

lines.speaker: Mai (finance manager)
lines.text: Perfect. What's the expected return on investment here?

lines.speaker: Duc (team lead)
lines.text: Faster rendering should save the team several hours a week.

lines.speaker: Mai (finance manager)
lines.text: Sounds reasonable. I'll start the approval process today.

questions.q: How many laptops does Duc want to buy?
What will Mai start after the discussion?

shadowing: I'd like to submit a purchase request for five new laptops.
Can you get a quotation from at least two different vendors?
This purchase counts as capital expenditure, not a regular expense.
Please attach a cost-benefit analysis before you submit the proposal.
What's the expected return on investment for this new equipment?
